name: Peanut Butter Mousse Cake
recipeIngredient: 10 peanut butter cups, mini, for garnish
recipeInstructions:
Heat oven to 350°F. Lightly grease an 8-inch square baking pan.
CAKE:
Mix flour, sugar, cocoa, baking soda, and salt in a medium-sized bowl.
Add yogurt, oil, and eggs; whisk until well blended. Pour into prepared pan.
Bake 40 minutes or until a pick inserted in the center comes out clean.
Cool in the pan on a wire rack for 15 minutes, then turn out onto a rack to cool completely.
MOUSSE:
While the cake bakes, put 1/3 cup of cream and peanut butter chips in a small saucepan.
Stir over low heat until the chips melt and the mixture is smooth. Remove from heat,
stir in vanilla, and let cool for 20 to 25 minutes.
Beat remaining 2/3 cup cream in a small bowl with an electric mixer just until soft mounds form when beaters are lifted.
Do not overbeat; the mousse won't be smooth if it's beaten too much.
Gently stir about 1/3 of the whipped cream into the peanut butter mixture until blended.
Then add the peanut butter mixture to the remaining whipped cream and gently stir until well combined without streaks.
Cut the cake into two layers using a long serrated knife. Place one layer on a serving plate and spread with mousse.
Top with the remaining cake layer. Refrigerate while preparing the glaze.
GLAZE:
Stir cream, butter, and sugar in a small saucepan over medium-high heat until the sugar dissolves.
Bring to a boil and add chips. Remove from heat; let stand for 5 minutes, then stir until smooth.
Pour glaze over the top of the cake and gently spread to the edges, letting it drip down the sides.
Refrigerate at least 2 hours or up to 1 day until set. Before serving, garnish with halved peanut butter cups.
Calories: 445
Fat: 28g
Exchanges: 2 starch, 1 fruit, 5-1/2 fat.
